headline: Meta's acquisition of a robotics startup signifies a strategic move to enhance its humanoid AI capabilities.

sections.title: News
items: Meta has acquired a robotics startup to advance its humanoid AI capabilities, potentially impacting sectors reliant on human-robot interactions.
Pentagon's new deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS aim to deploy AI on classified networks, signaling a push towards AI-security integration.
Anthropic might reach a potential $900 billion valuation round, expected within two weeks.

sections.title: Research
items: OmniRobotHome presents a multi-camera platform designed for real-time multiadic human-robot interaction (arXiv · Robotics).
FlexiTac offers an open-source, scalable tactile sensing solution for robotic systems (arXiv · Robotics).
LaST-R1 explores reinforcing action via adaptive physical latent reasoning for VLA models (arXiv · Robotics).

sections.title: Startups
items: Legora's $5.6B valuation marks its fierce competition with Harvey in the legal AI domain.
Replit’s Amjad Masad discusses strategic maneuvers against big tech like Apple, shedding light on startup resilience strategies.
xAI's new Grok 4.3 launch boasts a competitive price and includes a voice cloning suite, indicating a move to disrupt AI service pricing.

action_item: Explore opportunities or potential partnerships with Meta to integrate advanced humanoid AI capabilities into your robotics projects.
